docs.hero.issue.agent.label: Issue 02
docs.hero.issue.agent.text: Agents cannot tell which visible node is safe to edit.
docs.hero.issue.ir.label: Issue 01
docs.hero.issue.ir.text: Design IR drifts away from the shipped web files.
docs.hero.issue.review.label: Issue 03
docs.hero.issue.review.text: Reviews happen on exported artifacts instead of source changes.
docs.hero.lead: OpenGraphite opens the web source files themselves, keeps editor metadata in data-og-* and design values in --og-* variables, and makes HTML/CSS/assets/locales the reviewable deliverable instead of an exported copy.
docs.hero.title: Design tools, code, and agents should not edit different sources of truth.
docs.list.app.body: Web source files are the editable source of truth. The app layers editing affordances on top of WebKit instead of converting pages into a separate design IR.
docs.list.app.title: Design philosophy
docs.list.cli.body: Agents should inspect the .ogp scope, query nodes, make node-level edits, validate, and capture screenshots before handing work back.
docs.list.cli.title: Agent workflow
docs.list.css.body: Tags carry meaning, data-og-* carries editor metadata, --og-* carries editable design values, CSS renders them, and .ogp stores only project metadata and preview mocks.
docs.list.css.title: Source-of-truth contract
docs.list.mcp.body: Pages may keep lightweight og-instance references. OpenGraphite.runtime.js expands them for preview; ogkiln build expands them for static distribution. Generated DOM is not the source.
docs.list.mcp.title: Runtime and build boundary
docs.nav.footer.label: System
docs.nav.title: OpenGraphite Documentation
docs.spec.commands.body: ogkiln covers contract reads, project registration, document context, page/component graphs, i18n resources, validation, build output, screenshots, and node-level edits through one repository-backed interface.
docs.spec.commands.title: Command surface
docs.spec.edits.body: Prefer small node operations over full-file rewrites. Writes fail on duplicate data-og-id or data-og-internal-id, invalid target scope, invalid editable attributes, or post-edit validation errors.
docs.spec.edits.title: Node edits
docs.spec.heading.title: The technical contract for editing OpenGraphite pages.
docs.spec.i18n.body: Binding text keeps readable HTML fallback plus data-i18n-key. The preferred source for localized text is public/locales/<locale>.json; preview mock state selects a variant but is not itself a text resource.
docs.spec.i18n.meta: i18n inspect / i18n resource set / text variant set
docs.spec.i18n.title: i18n resources
docs.spec.mcp.body: OpenGraphite MCP exposes resources and tools, but write tools delegate to ogkiln. AI clients therefore read the same project scope, node graph, diagnostics, screenshots, and write path as humans and CI.
docs.spec.mcp.title: MCP interface
docs.spec.nodes.body: Tag names carry human meaning, data-og-* carries editor metadata, --og-* carries editable design values, and OpenGraphite.css turns type, layout, role, and variables into browser rendering.
docs.spec.nodes.title: Editable nodes
docs.spec.place.body: component-placement hosts only belong in component canvases. Their display clone is not editable source; edits resolve back to the source component node, while placement-specific mock state is stored in .ogp previewContext.placementMocks.
docs.spec.place.title: Placement mocks
docs.spec.refs.body: Agents should target copied ogref values. Pages use ogref:page:chapterInternalID:pageInternalID, components use ogref:component:collectionInternalID:componentInternalID, and nodes add the node internal ID.
docs.spec.refs.meta: data-og-id for display / data-og-internal-id for identity
docs.spec.refs.title: Reference IDs
docs.spec.scope.body: .ogp stores repository resolution, htmlRoot, Chapters, Collections, page/component entries, canvas placement, viewport size, and preview mock state. It does not duplicate DOM structure or design values.
docs.spec.scope.title: Project scope
docs.spec.slots.body: Component masters live in Collection HTML with data-og-component-kind=master. Pages keep lightweight og-instance references and pass page-specific content through standard slot attributes into master data-og-slot targets.
docs.spec.slots.title: Components and slots
docs.spec.source.body: HTML is the page DOM, CSS renders shared rules, assets and locale JSON stay as implementation resources, and runtime or build output is derived. OpenGraphite does not create another design IR.
docs.spec.source.title: Source model
docs.spec.verify.body: Every design change should validate the .ogp graph, capture visual output, and when static deployment matters, run build to expand component instances and copy public assets into a no-runtime distribution.
docs.spec.verify.title: Verify and build
downloads.build.body: Install XcodeGen if needed, then build the app and CLI. After the build finishes, run validation once to check that the bundled sample is ready to open.
downloads.build.title: 2. Build and check the sample
downloads.hero.lead: Clone the repository, build it locally, and open the bundled sample project. Follow the steps below to move from setup to a running OpenGraphite workspace.
downloads.hero.title: Start using OpenGraphite locally.
downloads.repository.body: Choose a working folder, clone OpenGraphite, and move into the repository. Run the remaining commands from this location.
downloads.repository.title: 1. Clone the repository
downloads.sample.body: Launch OpenGraphite.app and choose Open Sample Project on the welcome screen. The sample opens the bundled pages so you can inspect and edit them immediately.
downloads.sample.title: 3. Open the sample project
home.component.build.body: Spacing, order, and state remain attached to the page, so judgment made by a designer and judgment made with assistance meet in the same place.
home.component.build.title: People and tools read the same decisions.
home.component.runtime.body: Instead of polishing a disposable mock and rebuilding it later, OpenGraphite keeps design work close to the shape that continues forward.
home.component.runtime.title: Bring the canvas closer to the outcome.
home.component.title: Do not give every tool a different truth.
home.hero.lead: OpenGraphite keeps the place you shape an interface close to the place it continues to live. It reduces throwaway copies and keeps product decisions in one flow.
home.hero.scope: See it -> refine it -> keep it in place
home.hero.title: OpenGraphite
home.preview.title: OpenGraphite workspace
home.principles.agent.body: When work spreads beyond what can be seen, decisions become vague. OpenGraphite values changes that can be checked from the current view.
home.principles.agent.title: Stay accountable to what is visible
home.principles.runtime.body: A change should leave its intent close enough to be read later. OpenGraphite keeps the reason and the visible result from drifting apart.
home.principles.runtime.title: Leave decisions where they can be read
home.principles.source.body: The result of shaping the interface remains available for the next step. OpenGraphite avoids disposable copies and keeps work in one flow.
home.principles.source.title: Design where the work will remain
home.principles.title: OpenGraphite keeps product work from splitting into another truth.
